data.title: Quantum Computing News: SandboxAQ Wins Pentagon Deal as $200 Million Flows Into Quantum Startups
data.description:
Welcome to Thursday's edition of quantum computing news. In this issue, we look at a major federal deal for SandboxAQ, Quantum Computing Inc.'s debut at CES 2026, a $100 million funding round for Quantum Art, and Europe's growing push for quantum security and independence. Let's dive in.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

SandboxAQ Lands Pentagon-Backed Deal

We begin with SandboxAQ, which signed a five-year deal with the U.S. Department of War to help the agency prepare for future cyber threats. The deal will use SandboxAQ's AQtive Guard system, which helps track and manage all cryptographic tools across the department.

This is a key step as the U.S. moves toward post-quantum cryptography. The aim is to replace today's encryption methods before future quantum machines can break them. The agreement builds on past work SandboxAQ did with a U.S. defense lab to prove it could spot and fix weak points in current systems.

The AQtive Guard platform will help the government gain a full view of its digital keys and dependencies. It also flags risks tied to older systems and gives insight into how to fix them. If it works as planned, more agencies could follow. The government wants to get ahead of threats that experts say could reach critical systems as soon as 2029.

Quantum Computing Inc. Plans Big Reveal at CES

Next, Quantum Computing Inc. (QUBT), also known as QCI, will showcase its light-based machines at CES 2026 in Las Vegas. The company's gear uses a chip made from thin-film lithium niobate, a material known for fast light control. The chip runs at room temperature and uses little power, which could help bring quantum tools to a wider group of users.

The company says its systems don't need users to be experts in quantum science. The demo will focus on route planning for drones, faster training for AI models, and smarter ways to model finances. All of this runs on photonic chips that aim to offer speed and savings in real-world work.

QCI manufactures these systems at its Tempe, Arizona, foundry using a 150-millimeter wafer line. The company wants to close the gap between lab demos and field-ready machines. Visitors can try the tech at CES on January 7 and 8. Remote demos will also be available.

Quantum Art Raises $100 Million to Scale Up

We continue with Quantum Art, an Israeli startup developing full-stack quantum computers based on trapped-ion qubits, which just raised $100 million in fresh capital. The money will help build its next chip, called Perspective, which aims to run 1,000 quantum bits. The company is focused on trapped-ion tech, a system known for precision and strong qubit control.

The round was led by Bedford Ridge Capital and brings total funding to $124 million. Quantum Art plans to build a multi-core chip that can split tasks across zones while keeping full control. It has also hit a new milestone with a 200-ion chain, which shows early signs of scale.

Quantum Art works with Nvidia (NVDA) on quantum code and recently joined a project to model road traffic using its tools. The company says its chip design helps cut the steps needed for key math tasks. This could give it a lead as more firms look to scale past a few hundred qubits.

SEALSQ Boosts Quantum Fund to $100 Million

Lastly, in Europe, SEALSQ (LAES) has more than doubled its Quantum Investment Fund to $100 million. The group plans to build a secure European quantum system that runs without help from outside providers. This push ties into a larger goal to launch a full digital corridor linking labs, data centers, and industry groups across the continent.

The company is putting capital into post-quantum chips, secure links via satellites, and identity tools backed by blockchain. It also aims to back new startups in France, Spain, and Switzerland. Projects include a new chip test site in Spain and secure satellite links in space.

SEALSQ also invested in a U.S.-based firm called EeroQ, which uses electrons on helium to make chips. The goal is to connect U.S. and EU efforts without risking exposure to outside systems. Company leaders say this work supports Europe's long-term plan to guard sensitive data and build digital systems with full regional control.

We used TipRanks' Comparison Tool to line up all the tickers mentioned in the piece alongside notable quantum stocks. It's a quick way to see how they stack up and where the field could be heading.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Is Meta's Shifting AI Strategy a Red Flag for Investors?
data.description:
Meta Platforms (META) has had a troublesome 2025 as it pushes toward its ambitious AI goals. The company's upcoming Avocado AI model has reportedly been delayed until early 2026. The setback comes amid leadership shake-ups and growing internal pressure, raising fresh questions about Meta's AI roadmap.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

For investors, the delay of Meta's Avocado AI model highlights internal challenges but doesn't alter its long-term AI prospects. Despite a shifting strategy and some uncertainty, Meta's scale and resources give it a strong advantage — though execution will be key in 2026. Year-to-date, META stock has gained over 12%.

Meta's AI Push Hits Roadblock

According to CNBC, Meta is developing a new Llama successor and advanced frontier AI model, internally codenamed "Avocado." Last year, Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg expressed strong optimism about the company's Llama family of AI models. However, the company is moving from open-source development with its Llama models to a proprietary approach for its new Avocado model. This shift comes after Llama 4's underwhelming launch in April, which failed to impress developers and led to significant internal changes, according to sources.

Sources further stated that Meta had expected the Avocado model to launch before the end of this year, but the timeline has slipped to the first quarter of 2026. The report says Avocado is running into training and performance-testing issues. Meta, however, denies any delays.

Should Investors Be Worried?

The delay of Meta's Avocado AI model shows the company is facing some internal challenges, but it doesn't change its long-term position in AI. For long-term investors, this is more of a short-term setback than a serious warning. Meta's AI strategy has changed a lot this year, and as 2025 ends, it still looks somewhat unsettled. Still, Meta's size, resources, and focus on AI give it a big advantage, but execution will matter more than ever in 2026.

Additionally, Meta has been working hard to strengthen its position in the AI race. The company's Avocado model is expected to be a key move in challenging OpenAI's (PC:OPAIQ) ChatGPT and Google's (GOOGL) Gemini. For the company, this isn't just another AI upgrade—it's a strategic reset aimed at reclaiming relevance in the AI race.

Earlier this year, Zuckerberg created a new group called Meta Superintelligence Labs (MSL) to speed up progress. He also brought in top researchers from OpenAI, Google, and Anthropic to move faster toward building superintelligent AI.

Is META Stock a Good Buy Now?

Turning to Wall Street, Meta's shares have a Strong Buy consensus rating on TipRanks. This is based on 36 Buys, six Holds, and one Sell assigned by Wall Street analysts over the last three months. Furthermore, the average META price target of $832.06 indicates a 28% growth potential from its current price.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Down Almost 50%: Analysts Expect a Rebound for These 2 Beaten-Down Stocks
data.description:
As the year winds down, investors are already scanning the landscape for stocks that can deliver gains in 2026. One place that often gets overlooked is the market's hard-hit names – the ones that have tumbled 40% or more. While these declines may scare off casual observers, seasoned Wall Street analysts know that some of the most compelling opportunities are buried in the rubble.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

The key is understanding the reason behind the drop. Some companies are genuinely struggling, but others have been dragged down by temporary setbacks, sentiment swings, or overly pessimistic reactions. In those cases, the selloff can create an appealing setup: beaten-down stocks that now offer both a discounted entry point and the potential to rebound as conditions improve.

With that framework in mind, we turned to the TipRanks database to pinpoint names that fit the bill – stocks that have suffered steep declines yet are attracting constructive analyst attention. Below, we break down two such contenders, each carrying a Strong Buy consensus rating from the Street.

Transcat (TRNS)

The first stock we'll look at is Transcat, an industrial support firm based in Rochester, New York. The company is not a household name, but it does provide a set of services that are vital in modern industry: testing and calibration for all sorts of sensitive equipment. Transcat has a network of testing labs across the US, and in Canada and Ireland, where customers can have their equipment tested and accredited according to the most exacting industry standards.

Transcat's services include both lab and on-site calibration, as well as quality control and instrument repair services. The company also handles pickup and delivery of equipment to be tested. One main selling point for Transcat is that the firm makes regulatory compliance easy for its clientele, allowing them in turn to offer guaranteed quality to their own customers.

The company has been in the testing and calibration business for over 60 years and works with more than 30,000 customers every year. Transcat offers more than 500 lines of testing and measurement equipment and even offers equipment rental and asset management services to support customers who want to conduct testing in-house.

We should note here that Transcat's shares have fallen by almost 50% so far this year. The hit to the stock has come in the wake of mixed earnings and margin compression. In the company's last reported quarter, fiscal 2Q26, Transcat reported a non-GAAP EPS of 44 cents. This figure was 8 cents per share lower than in the prior-year quarter, and it missed the forecast by a nickel. In addition, Transcat saw its adjusted net margin drop year-over-year from 7.1% to 5.1%.

Elsewhere in the earnings report, Transcat reported solid revenues. The top line of $82.27 million was up more than 21% year-over-year and came in $2.55 million better than had been expected.

For Craig-Hallum analyst Greg Palm, Transcat shows potential for soild growth, starting in the second half of its fiscal year 2026. Palm sees the current low share price as a chance for investors to buy into a fundamentally sound stock at a discount price.

"Management continues to expect that the service business will return to HSD% organic growth in the 2H, a marked improvement from recent quarters' results. Commentary suggests a continued normalization of the Solutions business as well as a number of new account wins recently, which gives some good visibility into better growth in the near-term," Palm opined. "We see continued improvement in the P&L and potential for additional positive earnings revisions. With the stock sitting close to 52 week lows, either investors aren't paying attention or aren't aware of the upcoming improvements."

To this end, Palm puts a Buy rating on TRNS shares, which he complements with a $111 price target – pointing toward a robust one-year gain of 110%. (To watch Palm's track record, click here)

Overall, Transcat has a unanimous Strong Buy consensus rating, based on 3 recent positive analyst reviews. The shares are currently priced at $52.95 and their $100 average price target implies a one-year upside potential of 89%. (See TRNS stock forecast)
data.author: Michael Marcus

data.title: Strategy CEO Compares MSCI's Bitcoin Exclusion Proposal to 'Penalizing Chevron for Holding Oil'
data.description:
Strategy (MSTR) is intensifying its public war with index provider MSCI (MSCI), with CEO Phong Le directly comparing MSCI's proposal to remove firms with large crypto holdings to a biased attack on the digital asset class. Le argued that excluding companies like Strategy, which holds the majority of its assets in Bitcoin, would be like "penalizing Chevron for holding oil" or removing timberland company Weyerhaeuser (WY) for holding wood.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

MSCI is consulting on a proposal to exclude Digital Asset Treasury companies (DATs) whose crypto holdings represent 50% or more of their total assets, arguing that they "exhibit characteristics similar to investment funds," which are typically ineligible for index inclusion.

Saylor's Firm Defends Operating Company Status

CEO Phong Le strongly pushed back on the "investment fund" characterization, calling MSCI's stance "misinformed and misguided."

Le stressed that Strategy is "100% an operating company" that was founded in 1989 and has been public since 1998, emphasizing its long history as a software business with a proper corporate structure. The core of the argument is that Strategy utilizes its Bitcoin treasury as productive capital, differentiating it from a passive investment vehicle.

Exclusion Threatens American Innovation

The CEO further warned that MSCI's proposal improperly injects policy views and bias into its neutral index construction at a critical time for the sector. He argued that the move would dangerously "stifle innovation" in the digital asset category.

Le drew a historical comparison, stating the exclusion is akin to index providers in,

  • The 1980s saying telecom companies shouldn't have invested in cell towers and spectrum.
  • Three years ago saying AI companies shouldn't invest in LLM labs and high-performance computing.

Le's comments coincide with the release of Strategy's formal letter to MSCI, which explicitly argues that the exclusion proposal biases the index against crypto as an asset class. The consultation remains open until December 31, with the conclusion due on January 15 next year.

Is Strategy a Good Stock to Buy?

Analyst sentiment toward Strategy (MSTR), the company formerly known as MicroStrategy, is rated as a Strong Buy, based on the consensus of 14 Wall Street analysts tracked in the last three months. Of these ratings, 12 analysts call it a Buy, two recommend a Hold, and zero recommend a Sell.

The average 12-month MSTR price target sits at $481.08. This target implies a massive upside potential of 160.55% from the last price.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 12/11/25 – U.S. Stock Futures Fall Despite Rate Cut as Oracle Results Raise AI Concerns
data.description:
U.S. stock futures fell early Thursday despite the Federal Reserve's 25-basis point rate cut. Software giant Oracle (ORCL) raised investor concerns about an AI bubble after issuing a weak revenue outlook and increasing its spending forecast.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 1.22%, 0.89%, and 0.44%, respectively, at 3:46 a.m. EST on December 11.

During Wednesday's regular trading session, all three major indexes closed higher following the Fed's rate cut decision. The Dow rose by 1.05%, the S&P 500 advanced 0.67%, and the Nasdaq increased 0.33%. The Federal Reserve lowered the federal funds rate to a 3.5% to 3.75% range but signaled a wait-and-watch approach for future policy moves.

Investors will now focus on today's weekly initial jobless claims report and U.S. international trade data.

On the earnings front, companies including Broadcom (AVGO), Lululemon (LULU), and Costco (COST) are scheduled to report results.

Notably, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was down, floating near 4.13%. WTI crude oil futures were trending lower, hovering near $57.87 per barrel as of the last check. Additionally, the Gold Spot U.S. dollar price decreased to nearly $4,214 per ounce on Thursday.

Elsewhere, European indexes opened mixed as renewed worries over AI spending weighed on sentiment.

Asia-Pacific Markets Trade Lower

Asia-Pacific markets traded mostly lower, tracking Wall Street's losses.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index fell 0.04%. In China, the Shanghai Composite declined 0.70%, while the Shenzhen Component dropped 1.24%. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei lost 0.90%, and the Topix fell 0.94%.

data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: YouTube (GOOGL) TV Plans Bring Lower Prices and New Risk for Netflix Investors
data.description:
Google (GOOGL) announced it will roll out new YouTube TV Plans in early 2026. The plans will split the current $82.99-per-month bundle into more than 10 groups organized around defined themes. The groups will include sports, news, family, and other sets of live channels. The goal is to bring the price down for users who do not want the full line of channels. Each plan will cost less than the current full bundle. The firm will also retain key features such as cloud DVR and multiview mode. These tools help set the product apart from basic on-demand apps.

  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

At the same time, the move comes as YouTube holds the largest share of TV viewing time in the U.S. As a result, a lower entry cost may bring in users who want live TV but do not want a high bill. In turn, it may help Google grow paid use without a large rise in spend on growth work.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Alphabet Stock (GOOGL) Falls as Scope of Waymo's Vehicle Recall Is Revealed
data.description:
The U.S. road safety watchdog has revealed that tech giant Alphabet's (GOOGL) autonomous driving unit Waymo pulled back 3,067 of its vehicles in November over a software problem. GOOGL shares edged lower early Thursday.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

Waymo has been in the news recently for driving past school buses instead of stopping behind them when their stop sign arms are extended, and their lights are flashing because children may be crossing the road.

To address the issue, Waymo issued the recall, updated the automated driving systems in the affected vehicles, and wrapped up all corrective measures by November 17, the U.S. National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) said on Thursday.

NHTSA Probes Thousands of Waymo Vehicles

The software issue dates back to September, when media reports emerged that one of Waymo's self-driving vehicles refused to stop for a parked school bus in Atlanta, Georgia, while students were disembarking. The incident prompted the NHTSA to launch an investigation into 2,000 of Waymo's vehicles in October.

Amid the probe, authorities in Texas disclosed that the incident had happened at least 19 times since the start of the school year. Specifically, the Austin Independent School District noted that five incidents took place in November, even after Waymo deployed software updates intended to fix the problem.

Subsequently, Waymo said it was recalling 1,200 of the vehicles and will continue to update its software to address the issue. However, the NHTSA on December 3 informed the company that it has until January 20 to respond to its query on the matter.

Waymo Zooms to 14 Million Rides in 2025

The school bus safety episode comes as Waymo has reported recording industry-leading 14 million rides this year, having expanded into new U.S. cities, including Miami, Austin, Atlanta, Los Angeles, San Francisco, Phoenix, and Nashville. It also recently secured approval to test its self-driving features in New York City.

Waymo competes with EV giant Tesla (TSLA) and Amazon's (AMZN) Zoox in the autonomous vehicle space. Ride-hailing companies such as Lyft (LYFT) and Uber (UBER) have also stepped into the race.

Is Google a Buy, Hold, or Sell?

On Wall Street, Alphabet's shares continue to hold a Strong Buy consensus rating from analysts. This is based on 29 Buys and seven Holds issued by 36 analysts over the past three months.

However, the average GOOGL price target of $320.15 reflects a marginal downside risk of 0.02%.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Quantum Stocks IONQ, RGTI, and QBTS Plunge — Should Investors Buy Now?
data.description:
U.S.-based quantum computing stocks — Rigetti Computing (RGTI), IonQ (IONQ), and D-Wave Quantum (QBTS) fell sharply in the latest trading session. After reaching new highs in mid-October, quantum stocks have entered a difficult stretch over the past few weeks, with sharp swings and renewed volatility.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

Despite the pullback, the long-term promise of quantum computing hasn't changed. The next phase will depend on how effectively companies can demonstrate technological progress, secure strategic partnerships, and manage cash in a challenging market.

What Lies Ahead for Quantum Stocks?

The quantum computing sector is shifting from an academic concept to a high-risk, high-reward investment story, driven by technical progress and rising government and corporate funding. Pure-play names like IONQ, RGTI, and QBTS remain highly volatile, each advancing on a different tech path with its key milestones to watch.

1. IonQ, which uses trapped-ion technology, remains the sector's momentum leader. IonQ recently reached 99.99% two-qubit gate fidelity — the highest reported in the world — showing how accurately its quantum systems can perform operations. The company says this breakthrough will power its next-generation 256-qubit systems planned for 2026. However, IONQ stock carries the highest valuation in the group, trading at a steep premium to current sales. Notably, IONQ has a forward price/sales ratio of 177.7, compared to the sector average of 3.55.

2. Rigetti Computing relies on superconducting quantum processors and is deeply tied to U.S. government and defense contracts. Its growth will depend on landing new multi-year agreements, as well as expanding access through major cloud platforms. This government dependence makes the stock sensitive to any missed milestones or contract delays.

3. D-Wave Quantum operates in a unique niche with its quantum annealing technology, which is geared toward optimization problems in areas like logistics and finance. Investor focus should be on customer growth and adoption of its hybrid solvers, especially within its U.S. government business unit. While D-Wave's specialization offers differentiation, the company remains highly volatile due to cash burn and past insider selling.

For investors, the risks are still very high, including sharp stock swings and possible share dilution. But the long-term upside is also significant, with analysts expecting strong sales growth if the technology continues to advance.

RGTI, QBTS, IONQ: Which Is the Best Quantum Stock, According to Analysts?

data.title: Analysts Cut Tesla (TSLA) Q4 Delivery Outlook as Demand Pullback Emerges
data.description:
Tesla (TSLA) saw record sales in Q3 as buyers used the last round of the federal EV tax credit. The credit lifted demand and pushed sales to a new peak. The effect has now faded, and according to analysts, Q4 is set to show a clear pullback.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

The Street's analysts now see a slower quarter. FactSet projects Q4 sales of 450,000 units, a 9% decline. Bloomberg projects 448,000 units, a 10% decline. A key tracker named Troy Teslike expects 406,000 units, which marks a drop of 18%. He points to weak lines at plants and soft demand.

At the same time, market odds tell a cautious view. Odds from event trades set the chance of 430,000 units or more at 41%. The wide view of 406,000 to 450,000 units shows that the range is still quite large.

Meanwhile, TSLA shares rose 1.41% on Wednesday, closing at $451.45.
data.author: Ran Melamed
